vulnerability.title: Configure Accepting Router Advertisements on All IPv6 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl co

triage_decision.reason: Disabling IPv6 router acceptance (net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0) is a standard sysctl hardening step that does not affect authentication, authorization, or filesystem layout. It only changes kernel networking behavior and is unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption unless the environment relies on IPv6 SLAAC via router advertisements.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; May break IPv6 stateless address autoconfiguration (SLAAC) if the network depends on router advertisements for address assignment.; Could affect IPv6-based services that expect RA prefixes.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to propose a fix for the given finding: "Configure Accepting Router Advertisements on All IPv6 Interfaces". The rule is sysctl_net_ipv6_conf_all_accept_ra. The finding ID is that. Severity 2. The host is localhost.

We need to produce a remediation that disables IPv6 router acceptance (net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0). This is a sysctl hardening step that does not affect authentication, authorization, or filesystem layout. It only changes kernel networking behavior and is unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption unless environment relies on IPv6 SLAAC via router advertisements.

Thus we need to set net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra to 0. Use sysctl -w to set it. The recommendation: add line to /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra = 0.

We need to output the exact commands in the specified format: inner <function=...></function> block must be nested within an <yntax> tags? The instructions: "Function calls MUST follow the specified format: an inner <function=...></function> block must be nested within an <yntax> tags." So we need to output something like:

<function=...>
....</function>

But we need to choose a function name? Probably the function is "sysctl" or something? Actually we need to set a sysctl parameter. The function could be "sysctl" with parameters: -w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0. So we need:

<function=sysctl>
<-w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0>
</function>

But we also need to wrap in <function> and </function> tags. So final answer:

<function=sysctl>
<-w net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_ra=0>
</function>

We must ensure no extra text. Let's produce exactly that.
